title: narcissus bulbocodium subsp. romieuxii
intro: Narcissus bulbocodium subsp. romieuxii, also known as the Romieux's bulbocodium or Romieux's jonquil, is a subspecies of the narcissus bulbocodium plant, a type of wild daffodil native to Europe. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. It has been reported to have health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and treating digestive problems. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including infusions, tinctures, and dried extracts, which can be used to treat various ailments.
